trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the prescribing clinician to weigh Hunter Williams' older email (2025-02-06): "Standard starting dose: 1 mg = 0.2 mL = 20 units" against Hunter Williams' newer library-video instruction, which is operative for this refresh (2026-03-29, pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5 @ 00:08:55, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5/): "I would start with 0.5 milligrams, also known as 500 micrograms." The newer library-video also preserves Hunter Williams' hedge (2026-03-29, pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5 @ 00:10:29, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5/): "maybe even start smaller, 250 micrograms and just see how you respond."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-29, pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5 @ 00:07:53, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5/): "common side-effects include facial flushing, nausea, and headaches."
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-29, pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5 @ 00:08:24, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5/): "the flushing, the nausea, or the headaches, those typically, in my experience, go away in like 10 to 15 minutes."
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-29, pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5 @ 00:09:59, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5/): "start with 500 micrograms or 0.5 milligrams and then if need to go up in dosage You can go up to two milligrams."
- Hunter Williams (2026-03-29, which-peptides-can-you-combine-in-the-same-syringe-dont-be-a-pin-cushion--819718fb @ 00:15:33, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/which-peptides-can-you-combine-in-the-same-syringe-dont-be-a-pin-cushion--819718fb/): "if you do too large of a dose you'll just get really flushed and really red like a really bad niacin flush and you will probably get nauseous. If you take too much, you maybe want to throw up."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Hunter Williams (2026-03-29, pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5 @ 00:07:53, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/pt-141-how-to-use-it-for-the-first-time-and-beyond--4f11c9f5/): "common side-effects include facial flushing, nausea, and headaches"; Taylor Williams (2025-06-20, VHZuOyJwopI @ 00:07:53.440, https://youtu.be/VHZuOyJwopI): "a lot of women and sometimes men say like when they inject PT-141, they get nauseous and sick from it. My reason behind that is I think a lot of it is dosing it too high."
